mandarin.concept: The primordial non-being from which all being arises; the inexhaustible void-source
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.1: 无名天地之始 'The nameless is the beginning of heaven and earth' — the Dao as unnameable origin
mandarin.confucian_reference: Yi Jing, Xici: 易有太极，是生两仪 'The Yi has the Great Ultimate, from which spring the two Forms' — taiji as the undifferentiated origin
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Madhyamaka śūnyatā (空 kōng): the emptiness that is not nothingness but the unconditioned ground of all conditioned arising

sanskrit.concept: The void/zero-source; the unmanifest ground from which all manifestation emerges
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Ṛgveda X.129 (Nāsadīya Sūkta): नासदासीन्नो सदासीत् 'Neither non-being nor being was' — the undifferentiated ground prior to existence
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Nāgārjuna's Mūlamadhyamakakārikā: śūnyatā as prajñāpāramitā — emptiness is the womb of compassion
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Brahman as nirguṇa (without attributes): तत् त्वम् असि 'Tat tvam asi' (Chāndogya Upaniṣad 6.8.7) — the unqualified absolute

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 62a: אית ולית 'what exists and what does not exist' — the dialectic of manifestation
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:2a: 'Before the beginning, the King was arrayed in en-sof (אין סוף)' — the infinite as absolute void-source

mandarin.concept: The first emergence, the primal boundary that distinguishes inside from outside
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.1: 有名万物之母 'The named is the mother of the ten thousand things' — naming as primal division
mandarin.confucian_reference: Great Learning (大学): 物有本末，事有终始 'Things have root and branch; affairs have end and beginning' — the primacy of beginning
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Avatamsaka Sutra: the first vāsanā (impression) that creates bhāvanā (becoming) — the initial boundary of delusion

sanskrit.concept: The seed-moment; the first boundary that contains all future potential
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad 1.1.7: बीजाङ्कुराविव 'like the seed and the sprout' — bīja as primordial container of becoming
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Abhidharma: citta-vṛtti (mind-moment) as pravṛtti (turning-forth) — the first intentional boundary
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.121: हिरण्यगर्भः 'Hiraṇyagarbha' — the golden womb/egg of first differentiation

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 38a: 'Seven things were created before the world — Torah, teshuvah, Gan Eden, Gehinnom, the Throne of Glory, the Holy Temple, and the name of Messiah'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:3a: 'The first utterance: ב (Bet) is בִּינָה (Binah), the palace built upon the primordial point' — Bet as the first container

mandarin.concept: The seed of journey; the implanted potential that carries the traveler forward
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.41: 上士闻道，勤而行之 'The superior person hearing the Dao, diligently practices it' — the seed of practice germinating
mandarin.confucian_reference: Doctrine of the Mean (中庸): 天命之谓性，率性之谓道 'What Heaven decrees is nature; following nature is the Way' — the seed of destiny within
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Tathāgatagarbha Sūtra: 如来藏 (rúláizàng) — the Buddha-nature as seed within all beings, the journey toward awakening

sanskrit.concept: Going, movement, the journey of the soul through saṃsāra toward liberation
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 3.8: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं 'Perform your prescribed duty' — karma-yoga as the journey of right action
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Mahāyāna: the bodhisattva's gamana through the bhūmis (grounds/stages) — the journey of awakening
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.113: पथिभिः 'pathways' — the Vedic emphasis on the paths (panthās) of sacrifice and knowledge

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Avot 2:2: 'The reward of a journey is proportional to the exertion' — Gimel as the camel carrying the burden of learning
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:2b: 'ג (Gimel) is גְּדוּלָה (Gedulah), the flow of loving-kindness that journeys from the infinite to the finite'

mandarin.concept: The gate, portal, threshold — the liminal space between inside and outside
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.1: 玄之又玄，众妙之门 'Mysterious again mysterious — the gate of all wonders' — the Dao as perpetual threshold
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 6.15: 谁能出不由户 'Who can go out except through the door?' — the necessity of proper passage
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Sanmen (三门): the three gates of liberation — 空门 (emptiness), 无相门 (signlessness), 无愿门 (wishlessness)

sanskrit.concept: The door, entrance, threshold; the six sense-doors (ṣaḍāyatana) through which consciousness enters
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa: dvāra as the doorway of sacrifice — the threshold between human and divine realms
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Dhammapada 274: 'This is the door to the deathless — those who know, enter' — dvāra as the Noble Eightfold Path
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda VII.55: द्वारौ 'the two doors' — the gateway of dawn/night as cosmic threshold

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Shabbat 31a: 'The world is like a corridor before the world to come; prepare yourself in the corridor that you may enter the banquet hall'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:7a: 'ד (Dalet) is דַּלְתָא (door) — the doorway through which the Shekhinah enters the lower worlds'

mandarin.concept: Vital breath, the animating force; the subtle substance that fills the space between heaven and earth
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 通天下一气耳 'The whole world is one breath' — qì as the universal vital force
mandarin.confucian_reference: Mencius 2A.2: 我善养吾浩然之气 'I am good at nourishing my vast flood-like qì' — the cultivation of moral vital energy
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Ānāpānasati: 安那般那 (ānbānnà) — mindfulness of breathing as the foundation of meditative being

sanskrit.concept: Life-breath, vital force; the five prāṇas as the matrix of embodied existence
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Chāndogya Upaniṣad 5.19: प्राणो वा अश्वस्यात्मा 'Prāṇa is indeed the soul of the horse' — breath as the carrier of being
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Ānāpānasati Sutta (MN 118): mindfulness of in-and-out breathing as the direct path to nibbāna
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.66: प्राण 'prāṇa' as Vāyu — the wind-god who is the breath of the cosmos

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 10b: 'The soul fills the body and the breath fills the soul; woe to the body whose soul departs'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:15b: 'ה (He) is the letter of breath; with it God breathed the soul into Adam — נִשְׁמַת חַיִּים (breath of life)'

mandarin.concept: Connection, linkage, the thread that binds separate things into continuity
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.25: 有物混成，先天地生 'There was something undifferentiated, born before heaven and earth' — the primal connection of all
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 1.4: 吾日三省吾身 'I daily examine myself three times' — the connecting thread of self-cultivation
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Indra's Net (因陀罗网 yīntuóluó wǎng): infinite connections where each jewel reflects all others

sanskrit.concept: Binding, connection, the thread that links moments in the chain of becoming
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 7.7: मत्तः परतरं नान्यत् 'There is nothing higher than Me; all is strung on Me like pearls on a thread' — bandha as cosmic connection
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Pratītyasamutpāda: the twelve-linked chain (nidānas) — bandha as conditioned co-arising
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.90: पुरुषसूक्त 'Purusha Sūkta' — the cosmic man from whom all beings are woven like a web

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Menachot 29b: 'God created the world with the letter ה but sustains it with the letter ו (Vav) — the connector'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:16a: 'ו (Vav) is the hook of the middle pillar (Amuda de-Emtza'ita), connecting heaven and earth, Tif'eret as the bridge'

mandarin.concept: Implement, vessel, tool; the instrument through which process unfolds
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.11: 三十辐共一毂，当其无，有车之用 'Thirty spokes share a hub; its emptiness makes the wheel useful' — the vessel as functional void
mandarin.confucian_reference: Doctrine of the Mean: 致中和，天地位焉，万物育焉 'When harmony is achieved, heaven and earth take their places' — the process of cosmic ordering
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Upāya-kauśalya: 方便 (fāngbiàn) — skillful means as the weapon of wisdom wielded by the bodhisattva

sanskrit.concept: Weapon, instrument; the divine weapon of knowledge that cuts through ignorance
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 4.38: न हि ज्ञānēन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते 'Nothing is as purifying as knowledge' — jñāna-astra as the weapon of purification
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Prajñāpāramitā as the 'sword of wisdom' (prajñā-khaḍga) that severs the roots of kleśa
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda VI.16: the vajra (वज्र) of Indra — the thunderbolt as the divine weapon against chaos

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Shabbat 63a: 'A weapon is called zayin after the letter ז, for it is the seventh letter and Shabbat is the seventh day' — completion through process
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:18a: 'ז (Zayin) is the arm with which the King wages battle against the forces of division' — the process of cosmic maintenance

mandarin.concept: Enclosure, to surround, the fence that defines the boundary of the sacred
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.50: 出生入死 'Going out is life, entering is death' — the enclosure of existence within the boundary
mandarin.confucian_reference: Mencius 4A.1: 仁，人之安宅也 'Humaneness is the secure dwelling of the person' — the enclosure of virtue
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Maṇḍala (曼荼罗 màntúluó): the sacred enclosure that maps the cosmic order, the boundary of transformative space

sanskrit.concept: Enclosure, mental modification, the turning/circling that defines the boundary of consciousness
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Yoga Sūtras 1.2: योगश्चित्तवृत्तिनिरोधः 'Yoga is the cessation of the turnings of the mind' — the enclosure of mental activity
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Abhidharma: āvaraṇa (covering/enclosure) — the cognitive fences that obstruct direct seeing
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Atharvaveda: vṛta (enclosed) — the cosmic enclosure that protects the sacrifice

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 8b: 'A person should make a fence around his words' — the protective enclosure of speech
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:20a: 'ח (Chet) is the fence of the garden — the eight threads of the brit milah that enclose the covenant'

mandarin.concept: The serpent; the coiling descent into manifestation, the downward flow of energy
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 蛇蚹蜩翼 'The snake sheds its skin' — transformation through cyclical descent and renewal
mandarin.confucian_reference: Yi Jing, hexagram 48 (井 Jǐng, the Well): the descent into the depths to draw the living water
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Nāga (那伽 náqié): the serpent beings who guard the Dharma, dwelling in the depths as protectors of wisdom

sanskrit.concept: Serpent, dragon; the coiled power of kuṇḍalinī descending through the subtle body
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Kuṇḍalinī as the sleeping serpent at the base of the spine; her descent/ascent through the six cakras
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Nāgarjuna: 'Dragon-Tree' — the serpent wisdom of emptiness that descends to liberate
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda II.12: अहि 'ahi' (serpent Vṛtra) — the dragon of chaos whose defeat allows the descent of the waters

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 31b: 'The nachash of the Garden was the yetzer hara (evil inclination) — the descent into duality'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:36b: 'ט (Tet) is the hidden letter of tov (טוֹב, good) — the serpent's coil that contains the hidden light of transformation'

mandarin.concept: The hand; the instrument of action, the pointer toward manifestation
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 指手 'pointing at the finger' — the hand as both actor and signifier beyond itself
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 4.11: 君子欲讷于言而敏于行 'The noble person is slow in speech but quick in action' — the priority of hand over mouth
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Mudrā (手印 shǒuyìn): the ritual hand gesture that seals the realization, the hand as embodiment of enlightenment

sanskrit.concept: Hand; the instrument of karma, the active extension of will into manifestation
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Katha Upaniṣad 3.3: आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि 'Know the Self as the charioteer' — hasta as the reins of the senses
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Karma-mudrā: the hand of action that accumulates merit; abhaya-mudrā (fearlessness) — the hand that removes fear
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.51: 'The hand of Indra' — the divine hand that slays Vṛtra and releases the waters

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sotah 37a: 'The hand that writes is the hand that acts; the yod is the smallest letter but contains all ten sefirot'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:21b: 'י (Yod) is the hand of the Holy One, the point (nekuda) from which all creation extends — the seminal action'

mandarin.concept: To cover, overturn, the palm that both shelters and transforms
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.36: 将欲歙之，必固张之 'Before contracting, one must expand' — the covering that precedes revelation
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 2.1: 为政以德，譬如北辰 'Governing by virtue is like the North Star' — the canopy of moral coverage
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Dharmakāya (法身 fǎshēn): the truth-body that covers all phenomena, the universal palm of Buddha-nature

sanskrit.concept: Hand, palm; the doer, the ray of light, the extending and covering power
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 3.9.26: 'The palm of Prajāpati covers all beings; from his palm they emerge and to it they return'
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Karuṇā-pāṇi: the hand of compassion that covers all suffering beings; the covering protection of the bodhisattva
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda VIII.48: the 'hundred hands' of the gods — the manifold covering powers of the divine

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 7a: 'The palm of God covers and protects; it is the vessel that receives prayer'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:24a: 'כ (Kaf) is the crown of the letter, the palm that covers the ten sefirot like a mother hen covers her chicks'

mandarin.concept: Teaching, instruction, the transmission that bridges knower and known
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.27: 善人者不善人之师 'The good person is the teacher of the bad' — teaching as the natural flow of wisdom
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 7.2: 默而识之，学而不厌 'Silently treasuring knowledge, learning without satiety' — the inexhaustible posture of learning
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Upadeśa (优婆提舍 yōupótíshě): the teaching discourse, one of the twelve aṅgas of the Dharma; the bridge of transmitted wisdom

sanskrit.concept: To hang down, extend, the tongue of the guru that extends teaching to the student
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Śvetāśvatara Upaniṣad 6.23: 'To the one who has the highest devotion for God and for the teacher as for God, to him the meaning becomes clear'
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Guru-śiṣya-paramparā: the unbroken lineage of teaching; lamed as the connecting thread of Dharma transmission
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.71: ब्रह्मोद्य 'brahmodya' — the contest of sacred knowledge as the original teaching

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Avot 1.4: 'Make yourself a rabbi and acquire a friend' — the double structure of teaching and learning
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:28a: 'ל (Lamed) is the tallest letter, reaching above the line — the heart (lev לב) that learns and teaches simultaneously'

mandarin.concept: Water; the yielding substance that flows to the lowest place, the matrix of transformation
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.8: 上善若水 'Highest good is like water' — water as the model of virtue; ch.78: 'Nothing in the world is softer than water, yet nothing is better at overcoming the hard'
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 9.16: 逝者如斯夫，不舍昼夜 'It passes just like this, not ceasing day or night' — Confucius watching the flow of the river
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Dharma as water (法水 fǎshuǐ): the cleansing flow of the teaching that washes away defilements; the river of becoming

sanskrit.concept: Water; the primordial matrix, the fluid medium of cosmic becoming
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Ṛgveda X.121: 'In the waters is the support of the universe' — āpas as the cosmic foundation
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Tathāgatagarbha compared to the ocean: 'All beings have the Buddha-nature as the ocean contains the precious pearl'
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda VII.49: the seven rivers (sapta-sindhu) as the channels of cosmic order; water as the primordial āpas

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Ta'anit 7a: 'Just as water flows from a high place to a low place, Torah flows only from a humble person'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:29a: 'מ (Mem) is the open mem, the womb of the sea — mayim that flow from Binah to give birth to all forms'

mandarin.concept: The center, middle, mediator; the axis that holds all in balance
mandarin.taoist_reference: Doctrine of the Mean (中庸 Zhōngyōng): 中也者，天下之大本也 'The Mean is the great root of the world' — centrality as the ground of reality
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 6.27: 中庸之为德也，其至矣乎 'The Mean as virtue — how perfect it is!' — the centrality of moral balance
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Madhyamaka (中观 zhōngguān): the Middle Way between existence and non-existence; the mediator position of emptiness

sanskrit.concept: Middle, center, mediator; the axis mundi that connects all levels of reality
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 2.2.3: 'The ātmā in the center of the heart, smaller than a grain of rice, yet containing all worlds'
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Madhyamā-pratipad: the Buddha's Middle Way between extreme asceticism and indulgence; the centrist path of liberation
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.154: Viṣṇu's three strides — the middle step is in the sky, mediating between heaven and earth

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 108b: 'The fish (nun) in the waters represents the tzaddik in the Torah — sustained by the living flow'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:30b: 'נ (Nun) is the drop (nunta) of seed that falls into the waters of Mem; it is the mediator between the upper and lower waters'

mandarin.concept: Pillar, column, support; the vertical prop that holds up the structure of existence
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 栎社树 'The oak tree of the village shrine' — the pillar that supports by being useless, wu-wei as support
mandarin.confucian_reference: Great Learning: 德者本也，财者末也 'Virtue is the root, wealth the branch' — the pillar of moral support
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Pillar samādhi (柱三昧 zhù sānmèi): the concentrated state that supports all dhyānas; the central pillar of meditation

sanskrit.concept: Pillar, post, prop; the support that holds up the structure of consciousness
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Atharvaveda X.7: 'The pillar of the universe' — stambha as the axis of cosmic stability
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Bodhi-maṇḍa: the adamantine seat (vajrāsana) supported by the earth — the immovable support of awakening
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda IV.13: the skambha (scaffold/pillar) of the Atharvaveda that contains the whole universe within it

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 13b: semikhah — the laying on of hands as transmission of authority, the support of lineage
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:32a: 'ס (Samekh) is the prop of the fallen (סוֹמֵךְ נוֹפְלִים); it is Yesod as the pillar that supports the flow of blessing'

mandarin.concept: To see, perceive, insight; the eye that penetrates beyond surface appearance
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 见独 'seeing the unique' — the inner vision that beholds the One beyond differentiation
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 2.10: 视其所以，观其所由，察其所安 'See what he does, observe his motives, examine his resting place' — the gradations of seeing
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Darśana (达舍 dáshě): the direct seeing of truth; the eye of wisdom (慧眼 huìyǎn) that sees emptiness

sanskrit.concept: To see, the root of all vision; the seer, the seen, and the seeing as the triad of consciousness
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 13.6: 'The field and its knower' — dṛk (the seer) and dṛśya (the seen) as the two poles of perception
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Vipaśyanā (vipassanā): insight meditation as the clear-seeing of reality as it is; the three marks of existence
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.90: cakṣuṣī 'the two eyes' of the cosmic man — the sun and moon as the eyes of all

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 55b: 'The eye sees, but the heart understands' — ayin as the gateway to inner vision
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:35a: 'ע (Ayin) is the eye that sees not with light but through light — the mystical eye that beholds the Ein Sof'

mandarin.concept: Speech, word, language; the utterance that creates and names reality
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.56: 知者不言，言者不知 'Those who know do not speak; those who speak do not know' — the paradox of speech
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 13.3: 名不正则言不顺 'If names are not correct, speech will not be in order' — the rectification of names as foundation
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Vāc (语 yǔ): the power of sacred speech; dhāraṇī as the perfected utterance that holds the Dharma

sanskrit.concept: Speech, word, the creative utterance; vāk as the divine power that brings forth reality
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Ṛgveda X.125 (Devīsūkta): अहं राष्ट्री 'I am the Queen, the granter of wealth' — Vāk as the goddess of speech
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Mantra: the sacred syllable as vāc; oṃ as the primordial utterance containing all Buddhas
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Aitareya Brāhmaṇa: vāk as fourfold — parā, paśyantī, madhyamā, vaikharī — the four levels of speech

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Avot 1.17: 'Shimon his son said: All my life I grew up among the wise, and I found nothing better for the body than silence'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:37a: 'פ (Pe) is the mouth of the King (פֻּמָא דְּמַלְכָּא) — the opening through which the word of creation flows'

mandarin.concept: Righteousness, justice, proper conduct; the hunting of virtue, the pursuit of the moral way
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.18: 大道废，有仁义 'When the great Way is abandoned, there is humanity and righteousness' — yi as the remaining trace of the Way
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 4.10: 君子喻于义，小人喻于利 'The noble person understands righteousness; the petty person understands profit'
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Dharma as righteousness (法义 fǎyì): the pursuit of the noble truth; the hunt for liberation from saṃsāra

sanskrit.concept: Righteous, holy, good; the hunter of truth, the one who pursues the straight path
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Mahābhārata: sādhu-vṛtta — the conduct of the righteous; the pursuit of dharma as the great hunt
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Sādhu-puṇya: the merit of righteousness; the Four Noble Truths as the hunter's map to liberation
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.51: 'The straight path of the gods' — the ṛta as the cosmic righteousness pursued by the sage

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 6b: 'The tzaddik is the foundation of the world; his righteousness hunts down the divine sparks in exile'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:40a: 'צ (Tsade) is the righteous one who hunts (צַיָּד) for God in all things; it is Yesod as the channel of righteousness'

mandarin.concept: Transformation, change, the alchemy of becoming; the monkey as the trickster of change
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 庄周梦蝶 'Zhuang Zhou dreams he is a butterfly' — the transformation of identity; the monkey mind (心猿 xīnyuán)
mandarin.confucian_reference: Great Learning: 化民成俗 'Transform the people and complete the customs' — the transformative power of moral education
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Parināma (变化 biànhuà): transformation; the mind-monkey (心猿) tamed becomes the vehicle of enlightenment

sanskrit.concept: Transformation, ripening, change; the cosmic transformation from seed to fruit
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 2.13: देहीनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे 'As the embodied soul passes through childhood, youth, and age' — pariṇāma as the transformation of the body
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Parināma-vāda: the doctrine of transformation in Yogācāra — consciousness transforms from defiled to pure
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.90: 'From the sacrifice the verses and chants were born' — cosmic transformation through sacrifice

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 109a: 'The people of the generation of dispersion were like monkeys — each imitating the other, leading to confusion and transformation'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:42b: 'ק (Qof) is the monkey that mimics man; it represents the transformation of holiness into its opposite and back again'

mandarin.concept: Head, chief, beginning; the seat of awareness that governs the body
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.39: 神得一以灵 'The spirit attained the One and became luminous' — the head as the abode of the spirit
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 4.5: 君子去仁，恶乎成名 'If the noble person abandons humaneness, how can he be worthy of the name?' — the head that directs virtue
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Buddhāvataṃsaka: the jewel in the head of the king (顶髻 dǐngjì) — the ūṣṇīṣa as the summit of awareness

sanskrit.concept: Head, summit, crown; the highest point where awareness concentrates and radiates
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad 3.2.4: ātmā saṃprakāśate 'The Self shines forth' — śiras as the seat of the luminous Self
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Śikhara (峰顶 fēngdǐng): the peak of awareness; the crown of the head as the gateway to the highest consciousness
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.90: 'The head of the cosmic man became the sky' — śiras as the transformed awareness that becomes the heavens

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Pirkei Avot 4.15: 'Let the honor of your student be as dear to you as the honor of your colleague, and the honor of your colleague as the fear of your teacher, and the fear of your teacher as the fear of Heaven' — the hierarchy of the head
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:44a: 'ר (Resh) is the head (rosh) of all letters in the lower world; it is the awakening of awareness that precedes all directed action'

mandarin.concept: Change, transformation, mutation; the tooth that grinds and alters substance
mandarin.taoist_reference: Yi Jing: 穷则变，变则通 'When exhausted, change; by changing, there is passage' — change as the perpetual response
mandarin.confucian_reference: Doctrine of the Mean: 至诚无息，不息则久 'The utmost sincerity never ceases; without ceasing, it endures' — the change that is eternal
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Anitya (无常 wúcháng): impermanence as the fundamental mark of all conditioned existence

sanskrit.concept: To grind, whet, sharpen; the tooth of time that gnaws all things into change
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 2.16: नासतो विद्यते भावो 'The unreal has no existence; the real never ceases to be' — change as the hallmark of māyā
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Buddha's last words: 'All conditioned things are of a nature to decay — strive on with diligence' — anitya as the call to practice
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda I.113: 'The dawn changes everything' — śa as the grinding/transforming power of time

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Shabbat 151b: 'The tooth of time grinds all things' — shin as the agent of temporal transformation
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:46a: 'ש (Shin) is the tooth with three heads — the three lines of the patriarchs; it is the letter of change (שִׁנּוּי) that consumes the old and births the new'

mandarin.concept: Tally, token, seal, sign; the matching mark that certifies correspondence
mandarin.taoist_reference: Zhuangzi: 符验 'the tally of verification' — the sign that inner and outer correspond; the talisman of the Dao
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 2.1: 为政以德，譬如北辰 'Rule by virtue is like the North Star' — the unmoving sign around which all revolves
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Nimitta (相 xiàng): the sign of concentration; the mark (udāra) by which liberation is recognized

sanskrit.concept: Palm, mark, imprint; the trace left behind, the sign that proves presence
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Yoga Sūtras 1.30: the nine citta-vikṣepas (distractions) and their marks — tala as the trace of mental activity
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Mudrā: the imprint of the Buddha's hand on the rock; the three seals (trilakṣaṇa) as the marks of reality
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.71: 'The mark of the sacrifice' — the footprint of the divine on the earth of ritual

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Shabbat 55a: 'The mark (tav) of protection on the foreheads of the righteous' — the sign that distinguishes
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:48a: 'ת (Tav) is the seal of truth (תָּם); it is the final letter, the mark that completes and certifies all that came before'

mandarin.concept: End, completion, finality; the closing palm that gathers all into itself
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.16: 万物并作，吾以观复 'The ten thousand things arise together, and I watch their return' — the completion that is return
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 9.22: 苗而不秀者有矣夫 'Some sprout but do not flower; some flower but do not bear fruit' — the completion of the cycle
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Parinirvāṇa: 般涅槃 (bānìepán) — the final completion beyond birth and death; the last palm of the Buddha

sanskrit.concept: End, limit, boundary; the final extremity where the palm closes and completion is achieved
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 18.66: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य 'Abandon all dharmas and take refuge in Me alone' — the final surrender as completion
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Pari-nirvāṇa: the complete blowing-out; the final palm that closes the book of saṃsāra
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Chāndogya Upaniṣad: the end of the journey as return to the source — 'tat tvam asi' as the completion of all seeking

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Pesachim 50a: 'The final letter is the seal of the alphabet; from its palm all returns are gathered'
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:50a: 'The final כ is the palm that receives the Shekhinah at the end of days — it is Malkhut as the vessel that completes the cycle'

mandarin.concept: Sea, ocean; the final waters that gather all rivers, the completion of flow
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.32: 譬道之在天下，犹川谷之于江海 'The Dao in the world is like rivers and streams flowing to the sea' — the ocean as the final return
mandarin.confucian_reference: Mencius 2A.2: 源泉混混，不舍昼夜 'The spring gushes forth, never ceasing day or night' — all waters flowing to the great completion
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Sāgara (大海 dàhǎi): the ocean as the great storehouse; samudra as the unfathomable depth of Buddha-nature

sanskrit.concept: The gathering-together of waters; the ocean as the final receptacle of all flow
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 2.70: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं 'The ocean remains steady though filled by all rivers' — the completion of non-reacting
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Dharma-ocean (法海 fǎhǎi): the Buddha's teaching as the ocean that contains all dharmas; the final gathering
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda VII.49: 'the ocean, the gatherer of waters' — samudra as the completion of the seven rivers

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Ta'anit 9a: 'All waters flow to the Great Sea; all Torah flows to the final mem' — the completion of wisdom
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:52a: 'The closed mem at the end is the womb that has given birth; it is the ocean of Binah that has released all her waters'

mandarin.concept: Return, go home, terminal convergence; the fish returning to the deepest water
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.16: 夫物芸芸，各复归其根 'The myriad creatures all return to their root' — the terminal return as completion
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 1.9: 慎终追远 'Be careful in the end and remember the distant past' — the terminal as the gateway to origin
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Parinirvāṇa as guī: the final return to the source; the fish (nun) that has completed its upstream journey

sanskrit.concept: Final return, turning back, terminal revolution; the completion of the cycle
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 8.21: गतिः 'That is the highest goal from which one never returns' — the terminal beyond return
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Nirvāṇa with no remainder (nirupadhiśeṣa): the final extinguishing where no fish remains to swim
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.135: the path of the ancestors (pitṛyāṇa) and the path of the gods (devayāṇa) — the terminal fork

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Berakhot 3b: 'The final nun is the fish that has descended to the lowest depth; from there it rises again' — terminal as turning point
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:54a: 'The long nun at the end stretches below the line — it has descended to the terminal point and now reaches upward for renewal'

mandarin.concept: Silence, wordlessness, the final mouth that closes into perfect stillness
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.56: 知者不言，言者不知 'Those who know do not speak; those who speak do not know' — the silence that knows
mandarin.confucian_reference: Doctrine of the Mean: 莫见乎隐，莫显乎微 'Nothing is more visible than what is hidden, nothing more apparent than what is minute' — the silence of the subtle
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Noble Silence (圣默然 shèng mòrán): the Buddha's silence before ultimate truth; the silence of Vimalakīrti

sanskrit.concept: Silence, the vow of silence; the final mouth that closes into the fullness of being
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Chāndogya Upaniṣad: the silence of the Self — 'the space within the heart' (dahara-ākāśa) where no word enters
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Ārya-tūṣṇīm-bhāva: the noble silence of the Buddha; mauna as the completion of all discourse in direct realization
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.129: 'Who knows, who can declare?' — the silence of the unknowable origin

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Pirkei Avot 3.13: 'Silence is a fence for wisdom' — the final pe as the enclosure that protects the deepest truth
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:56a: 'The final פ is the mouth that has spoken all and now closes; it is the seal of silence upon the completed word'

mandarin.concept: Upright, correct, justice; the final righteousness that rights all wrongs
mandarin.taoist_reference: Dao De Jing ch.45: 清静为天下正 'Tranquility is the rectifier of the world' — the stillness that restores justice
mandarin.confucian_reference: Great Learning: 正心诚意 'Rectify the heart and make sincere the will' — the foundation of cosmic justice
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Samyak (正见 zhèngjiàn): right view as the first of the Noble Eightfold Path; the hunt that finds the ultimate truth

sanskrit.concept: Cosmic order, truth, righteousness; the final justice that restores the original harmony
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Ṛgveda I.51: ऋतं 'ṛta' — the cosmic order that the gods protect; the justice underlying all sacrifice
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Samyañc (正见): the rightness that is the culmination of the eightfold path; the final hunt that captures the truth
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.190: 'Ṛta and satya are established in heaven' — the primordial justice that is the ground of all

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 90a: 'In the future world, the righteous will sit with crowns on their heads and enjoy the radiance of the Shekhinah' — the completion of justice
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:58a: 'The final צ is the tzaddik who has completed the hunt; it is Malkhut as the executor of divine justice, the straight line that rights all crookedness'

mandarin.concept: Return, repeat, recursion; the turning-back that is also a new beginning
mandarin.taoist_reference: Yi Jing, hexagram 24 (复 Fù, Return): 反复其道，七日来复 'Returning along its way, in seven days it returns' — the cyclical return as recursion
mandarin.confucian_reference: Analects 1.4: 吾日三省吾身 'I daily examine myself three times' — the recursive practice of self-cultivation
mandarin.buddhist_reference: Saṃsāra-cakra (轮回 lúnhuí): the wheel of becoming as recursive return; each cycle transforms the traveler

sanskrit.concept: Recurring, turning again, the recursive loop that spirals upward through each cycle
sanskrit.hindu_reference: Bhagavad Gītā 8.19: भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते 'Repeatedly being born, it repeatedly dissolves' — the cosmic recursion of prakṛti
sanskrit.buddhist_reference: Bhava-cakra: the wheel of existence as a recursive structure; each return deepens wisdom if awareness is present
sanskrit.vedic_reference: Ṛgveda X.90: the cosmic sacrifice that must be repeated eternally — punarāvṛtti as the sustaining rhythm of reality

ancient_hebrew.talmudic_reference: Sanhedrin 97b: 'The world is destined to exist for six thousand years; the seventh is for destruction, and then it is made new again' — cosmic recursion
ancient_hebrew.zohar_reference: Zohar I:60a: 'The end is wedged in the beginning (סוֹף מַעֲשֶׂה בְּמַחֲשָׁבָה תְּחִלָּה) — the reset is the return to the point before the point, the recursion that generates all worlds'
